basics.name: Sudhakar Reddy Peddinti
basics.label: Staff Software Engineer – Platforms & Search Systems
basics.summary: Staff Software Engineer with 8+ years building Java/Spring Boot microservices that power search and product-discovery platforms at a top-3 global retailer. Owned the full lifecycle of 7 production services — from API design and data-layer migration through deployment, observability, and on-call — across 3,200+ commits and 28 repositories. Specialist in polyglot data architectures (PostgreSQL, Cassandra, CosmosDB, ElasticSearch), multi-tenant platform onboarding, and zero-downtime schema migrations. Consistently elevated service ownership from feature delivery to architecture strategy, cross-team contract definition, and platform-wide modernization.

work.name: Large-Scale Retail E-Commerce Company
work.summary: Lead architectural strategy for a platform of 22+ backend services that control search ranking, faceted navigation, and merchandising rules for 10+ global markets. Own cross-team API contracts, multi-tenant onboarding roadmaps, and org-wide infrastructure modernization.
highlights: Designed and executed the multi-tenant platform onboarding for the Item-Pinning service: merged divergent US and international Spring Boot codebases into a single deployable, introduced tenant-aware DAO layers with dual-write to both AzureSQL and GCP PostgreSQL for A/B cut-over, provisioned per-tenant Cassandra and distributed-cache clusters, and rolled out 5 tenants across 27 milestones — all with zero downtime.

Architected the Facet Lifecycle Management backend (Discovery Service + Facet Manager): designed 8 RESTful endpoints covering impact analysis, async deactivation, CSV export to Azure Blob Storage, and automated expiration with a state machine (PUBLISHED → UNPUBLISHED → EXPIRED) preserving blacklist semantics — operating over 82,000 curations and 2,287 facet definitions backed by CosmosDB and ElasticSearch.

Co-designed a paginated v2 read path for the Facet Sync pipeline (SFCS): defined per-rule branching logic (upsert/skip/delete), a Cassandra TTL contract (864K-second prod / 86.4K-second non-prod), idempotent delete handling, and a phased CCM feature-flag rollout — eliminating ~500 LOC of legacy JOLT transformation code and a class of stale-data tombstone race conditions.

Spearheaded platform-wide Spring Boot 2→3 migration, CentOS EOL container removal, CCM2 client integration, and Akeyless secrets rotation across 22+ services — closing multiple CVEs, removing all hardcoded credentials, and standardizing P0 service health-check patterns for Kubernetes readiness/liveness probes.

Built a Python-based AI diagnostic tool (Sherlock) for automated search-quality investigation: integrated with internal Sunlight debug UI, search-cache metadata APIs, and Polaris runtime data — authored the definitive service-interaction map between 10+ platform and runtime services, adopted as the standard onboarding reference.

work.name: Large-Scale Retail E-Commerce Company
work.summary: Took full technical ownership of the core rules-management backend (1,261 commits, 81K+ lines) and expanded scope to system design, data-model migrations, and experimentation infrastructure across 12 active repositories.
highlights: Designed and shipped the V2 rules engine — replaced a multi-table legacy schema with a single-table versioned model keyed on <query, target_section>, implemented Spring-managed migration jobs to convert 58K+ rules from v1→v2 format, and led a 4-phase production rollout with per-phase CCM switches and automated regression gates.

Built the Affinity Override Service end-to-end (299 commits): Spring Boot REST API with Azure Blob Storage integration for bulk carousel data, generics-based DAO to reduce duplicate data-access code (identified via SonarQube), performance load testing, and Phase-2 enhancements including stale-category fix and stage-based routing logic.

Engineered interleaving experiment infrastructure for A/B testing search-ranking rules: added an interleaving field to the shared Cosmo DB Util library, wired Perceive/Guide runtime feature flags via Polaris USGM config, built email notification for expired and zero-result rules via Concord scheduled jobs, and implemented rule-explosion control to cap combinatorial growth.

Executed the Guru PostgreSQL migration (AzureSQL → GCP Cloud SQL): authored the full migration playbook, refactored tenant-specific Couchbase connection logic, added a 3rd WCNP cache cluster for HA, upgraded to Spring Boot 2.7, and removed legacy SBMS integration and hardcoded secrets — drove the same upgrade pattern across Hulu and Item-Pinning services.

Built the Category Intervention Dashboard (CSID) as a standalone Spring Boot service: separated it from the Guru monolith, implemented category-impact APIs, batch-update endpoints, CSV data-export, and daily-trends Concord jobs — the tool became the primary operator interface for 100+ internal specialists.

Coordinated emergency Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) remediation across 4 Java services within 48 hours — upgraded log4j-core to 2.17.0, resolved transitive dependency conflicts in Maven BOM hierarchies, and verified fix across all deployment environments.

work.name: Large-Scale Retail E-Commerce Company
work.summary: Joined the Search Platform team and contributed 175+ commits in year one across 4 repositories — building core backend services for search-curation and item-ranking tooling from the ground up.
highlights: Developed the Item-Pinning Service from early-stage (582 total commits over tenure): implemented normalized-query API integration with the ranking runtime, resolved Couchbase connection pooling bottlenecks (NullPointerException in empty-string handling, CB dependency exclusions), and delivered OG tenant configuration — establishing the service that would scale to support 5 global tenants.

Built the Manual Shelves Syncer service from scratch: designed Spring Boot REST APIs for audit logging, page-count queries, and delta-sync capabilities; authored the PagerDuty alerting runbook and operational playbook still in active use; service handles full and incremental shelf-data synchronization for merchandising categories.

Implemented Concord CI/CD pipeline automation for 6+ scheduled platform jobs — data priming, rule expiration notifications, and reporting triggers — establishing YAML-based configuration patterns and Concord library versioning practices adopted across the team's entire service portfolio.

Contributed foundational Guru API development (Orion/OOS exception management endpoints, Couchbase-backed data models, Splunk log routing with switch-based logic) and created the OCRA troubleshooting guide used during on-call rotations.

Initiated CSID module separation from the Guru monolith: added product-type column support, built the database schema for category-specialist interventions, and laid the architectural groundwork for its later extraction into a standalone service.

work.name: Accenture
highlights: Proposed and implemented database query pruning techniques that reduced data query execution times to under 35 seconds.
Designed and developed application enhancements and bug fixes for high-impact customer applications using Java, Hibernate, and Spring MVC.
Conducted rigorous problem management and root cause analysis, receiving the Best Performance award for resolving critical production issues.
Built and managed CI/CD automation pipelines using Jenkins, Maven, and SVN to support stable patch rollouts.

work.name: University of Missouri - Kansas City
highlights: Conducted data analytics and statistical processing to extract meaningful research insights.
Developed a React.js and Redux-based web dashboard for high-performance interactive data visualization utilizing Google Charts.
Leveraged AWS Services (S3, Lambda, API Gateway) and Node.js to design serverless backend APIs.

skills.name: Observability & Reliability
keywords: CVE Remediation (Log4Shell, Postgres libs, CentOS EOL)

key_differentiators.summary: Why hire me as a Staff Platform Engineer

points.detail: I have 3,200+ commits across 28 repositories over 8 years — not as a contributor, but as the primary owner. I have built 4 services from scratch, migrated 3 data layers (Couchbase→Cassandra, AzureSQL→PostgreSQL, legacy→CosmosDB), and maintained production SLAs throughout. I understand Java/Spring Boot at the level of Maven BOM conflicts, ThreadLocal connection-pool tuning, and log4j transitive dependency resolution.

Every migration I have delivered — Presto v1→v2 (58K rules), Couchbase→MeghaCache+Cassandra, AzureSQL→GCP PostgreSQL, 5-tenant WCP onboarding — used dual-write DAOs, CCM feature flags, and phased rollouts to guarantee zero downtime. I treat safe migration as a first-class engineering discipline, not an afterthought.

Beyond my owned services, I contribute to the shared library (cosmo-db-util), CI/CD pipeline configs (161 Concord commits), regression automation (59 commits), Grafana/Prometheus dashboards (28 commits), and runtime feature-flag configurations. I operate as a platform engineer across the full stack — from API contract to Kubernetes probe tuning to Kafka consumer-lag alerting.
